comment.body:
Very risky window… this whole Q3/Q4 window. Investors are cashing out, and doing speculative trades, while money is flowing into bonds and MMFs.
It is literally impossible to have significantly higher odds for any outcomes now, this is why it is called “uncertainity”, bcs the value of odds are near to each other…

comment.body: The bottom line (without any intelligence info) is, the market is visibly fragile (up by mechanical flows, squeezes etc.), expensive, concentrated and overextended, making it prone to risks. Now, if Trump could pull off the truce then it will be the new story of a bull market, and we won't be late; otherwise if the truce fail, then Trump would be in trouble.

comment.body:
Alma, I’m in the process of delving into RNN’s for my current model, specifically Long-Short Term Memory (LSTM) and from what I’m understanding, this RNN seems to be much more dynamic than an HMM model for not only detecting regimes but understanding the nuances of the regime as well. I’m looking specifically at mLTSM within a Bayesian NN as a possibility in the future but I’m wondering why a quant would choose to use a normal HMM model instead of something like this? 

Really looking forward to the ML educational post in the future. This stuff is incredible and I hope you include all of the math with it as well
